definitions.definition: The smallest unit of matter that still retains the properties of an element. It consists of three main subatomic particles: protons, neutrons, and electrons.
definitions.example: Hydrogen atom consists of one proton, one electron, and no neutrons.

definitions.definition: A positively charged subatomic particle present in the nucleus of an atom. It has a mass of approximately 1 atomic mass unit.
definitions.example: A hydrogen atom has one proton in its nucleus.

definitions.definition: A subatomic particle with no charge, present in the nucleus of an atom. It has a mass of approximately 1 atomic mass unit.
definitions.example: An atom of helium has two protons and two neutrons in its nucleus.

definitions.definition: A negatively charged subatomic particle that orbits the nucleus of an atom. It has a negligible mass compared to the nucleus.
definitions.example: An electron orbits the nucleus of a hydrogen atom.

definitions.definition: The number of protons present in the nucleus of an atom, which determines the identity of an element.
definitions.example: The atomic number of oxygen is 8.

definitions.definition: The total number of protons and neutrons present in the nucleus of an atom.
definitions.example: The mass number of oxygen is 16.

definitions.definition: Atoms of the same element with the same number of protons but different numbers of neutrons.
definitions.example: Hydrogen has two isotopes: protium and deuterium.

definitions.definition: The central part of an atom, consisting of protons and neutrons, which contains most of the atom's mass.
definitions.example: The nucleus of a hydrogen atom contains one proton.

definitions.definition: A unit of mass equal to 1/12 the mass of a carbon-12 atom.
definitions.example: The atomic mass unit is used to express the mass of subatomic particles.

definitions.definition: A region around the nucleus of an atom where electrons are likely to be found. Each shell has a specific energy level.
definitions.example: The first electron shell of a hydrogen atom contains one electron.

definitions.definition: A region around the nucleus of an atom where an electron is likely to be found, described by three quantum numbers: n, l, and m.
definitions.example: The electron orbital of a hydrogen atom is spherical in shape.

definitions.definition: A set of three numbers that describe the energy, shape, and orientation of an electron orbital in an atom.
definitions.example: The quantum number n = 1 corresponds to the first electron shell.

definitions.definition: An electron in the outermost energy level of an atom that participates in chemical bonding.
definitions.example: The valence electron of a hydrogen atom is the single electron in the first energy level.

definitions.definition: The number of electrons an atom can gain, lose, or share to form a chemical bond.
An atom or group of atoms with an electric charge due to the gain or loss of electrons.
definitions.example: A sodium ion has 11 protons and 10 electrons.

definitions.definition: A positively charged ion formed when an atom loses one or more electrons.
definitions.example: A sodium cation has 11 protons and 10 electrons.

definitions.definition: A negatively charged ion formed when an atom gains one or more electrons.
definitions.example: A chloride anion has 17 protons and 18 electrons.
